Defective Fishplate: A fishplate is a flat piece of metal used to join two lengths of railway track. A defective fishplate often has visible damage, misalignment, or missing connectors.
Fastener: Fasteners secure the rail to the railroad ties. They typically appear as small, noticeable clamps or clips attached directly to the rail.
Missing Fastener: This class describes the absence of fasteners where they should be present along the rail.
Non Defective Fishplate: A non defective fishplate is a properly aligned and undamaged connector joining two rails.
